summary: The prior round had no findings, and this round remains clean. The PLAN-side orphan enumeration is pinned to a syntactically validated configured App bot identity, while CLAIM compares that exact configured identity with the successfully minted App slug before trusting the plan accounting. The v5 orphan rows are inert, strictly field- and value-validated, bounded before log/summary use, restricted to planned repositories, deduplicated, and deterministically ordered. Enumeration mirrors the review lane's worker-shape and provenance-admission gates, excludes human-held PRs, and retains malformed-head cases via a safe sentinel. The production workflow forwards both identity values, counts orphans as outstanding work, and surfaces them without adding a mutation path. The self-tests include meaningful positive and negative cases for schema validation, enumeration, pin divergence, and live workflow wiring. I found no correctness, soundness, fail-closed, security, or test-validity defect in the diff.
